stories: Good morning Sean, it's Sunday, the nineteenth of April, 2026
Here's a little fun to start the day: on this exact day back in 1987, The Simpsons first appeared on television — not as their own show, but as a scrappy animated short on The Tracey Ullman Show
Your calendar is looking clear today — a proper Sunday, no meetings pencilled in, so it's yours to spend as you like
Over in Singapore, two stories worth your attention
Singtel pointed the finger at on-site construction works disrupting all operators at once, a reminder of how much of the city's connectivity sits on shared infrastructure underground
Second, the Circle Line saga is winding towards a happier chapter
LTA and SMRT finished the three-month tunnel strengthening works nine days ahead of schedule, and they're now in final systems testing for the Circle Line Stage 6 opening
Services still end earlier on Saturday nights and start later on Sunday mornings through mid-May to allow for testing, so if you're planning an early ride today, the CCL doesn't run until nine

Good morning Sean, it's Saturday, the eighteenth of April, 2026
On this exact day back in 1930, the BBC's evening news bulletin opened with the announcer saying, quite seriously, "there is no news"
The broadcaster simply played piano music for the rest of the fifteen-minute slot
Today we don't quite have that luxury, so let's get into it
Looking at your day, you're over in Saskatoon at the Sheraton Cavalier, marked as out-of-office and working Canadian hours
Your calendar's got a morning run pencilled in and otherwise a do-not-disturb block through the working day, so it looks like a focused one
Over in Singapore, a couple worth your attention
A postgraduate student was taken to hospital after a fire broke out in a research laboratory at Nanyang Technological University yesterday afternoon
